frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: Attractive bias of current estimate toward immediately preceding stimulus.

frameworks.name: short-term synaptic facilitation
frameworks.one_line: Fast plasticity proposed as serial-dependence substrate, deemed too fast here.

frameworks.name: metaplasticity
frameworks.one_line: Slower plasticity mechanism invoked to explain session-long build-up.

frameworks.name: natural-scene-statistics adaptation
frameworks.one_line: View that biases track stable environmental correlations.

core_question: Does serial dependence arise for color working memory, and how does its strength evolve within a session?
key_assumptions: Sequential trial stimuli were temporally uncorrelated by design.
manipulations: Trial position within an experimental session.
Pooling across 8 independent color delayed-estimation datasets.
key_findings: Significant attractive serial dependence in 7 of 8 color datasets (n=760).
Strength of serial dependence built up gradually across the session.
interpretations: Build-up implies a slower, metaplastic mechanism beyond short-term facilitation.
Uncorrelated stimuli argue against ongoing adaptation to environmental statistics.
